subject: Computer Networking
title: Content Delivery Networks
description: A micro-lesson that makes Content Delivery Networks usable.

hook.text: Your server is in Virginia. A user in Tokyo waits 200ms per request. A CDN serves from Tokyo instead.

buildup.text: CDNs cache content on edge servers worldwide. Users connect to the nearest one for faster delivery.

discovery.text: Static assets — images, CSS, JS — are cached globally. The origin server is hit only on cache misses.

twist.text: Modern CDNs run code at the edge: Cloudflare Workers and Vercel Edge Functions compute near the user.

climax.text: CDNs also absorb DDoS attacks. Distributed infrastructure can handle traffic spikes your origin can't.

punchline.text: CDNs put your content everywhere. Closer to the user means faster.

quiz.question: What is the main benefit of a CDN?
options: Serving content from servers closer to the user
Encrypting all data on the origin server
